title: volume export: refuse to write to terminal (TTY)
body:
### What I did
- Fix #26506

### How I did it
Add targetPath-and-isTerminalSTDOUT check at the very beginning in `export.go`(`func export`), and error is expected if true.

### How to verify it

1. Existing Positive Case: podman volume export with redirect. 
2. New Positive Case: 

- podman volume export with output flag in TTY by `script` command, it should return exit code=0

3. New Negative Case: 

- podman volume export without flags in TTY by `script` command, error is expected.


 **Why not add a test case in `450-interactive.bats` where `$PODMAN_TEST_PTY` code pattern can be reused?**

1. Add a test in `450-interactive.bats`
```bats
@test "podman volume export refuses to write to STDOUT if it is a TTY" {
    ...
    echo "stdin  -> $(readlink /proc/$$/fd/0)"
    echo "stdout -> $(readlink /proc/$$/fd/1)"
    echo "stderr -> $(readlink /proc/$$/fd/2)"

    run_podman 125 volume export testVol <$PODMAN_TEST_PTY

    is "$output" "Error: refusing to export to terminal. Use -o flag or redirect" "Should refuse to export to terminal"
    ...
}
```

2. Result shows that stdout is not from TTY. That's why test failed.
```
[22:54:14.485829810] # /home/arthur/podman/bin/podman run -v testVol:/data --rm quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 sh -c echo hello > /data/myfile
   stdin  -> /dev/pts/1
   stdout -> /tmp/bats-run-vyervr/test/5.out
   stderr -> /tmp/bats-run-vyervr/test/5.out
   
   [22:54:15.083204283] # /home/arthur/podman/bin/podman volume export testVol
   /usr/local/lib/bats-core/test_functions.bash: line 380: warning: command substitution: ignored null byte in input
   [22:54:15.146145889] myfile0000644000000000000000000000000615030125326010445 0ustar0000000000000000hello
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #| FAIL: exit code is 0; expected 125
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   # [teardown]

```


**Another way to write test**
1. Use `script` to open a new pesudo-terminal(PTY) to make podman think it's running in a terminal, and save its exit code manually
```sh
all_outputs=$(script -q -c "$PODMAN volume export testVol; echo \$?" /dev/null)
```
2. There will be two lines, 1st is error message, 2nd is exit code.
3. However, since the output is produced via `script` command, each line ends with `\r\n` (`0D 0A` in hex). We need to trim them...
```sh
[arthur@localhost podman]$ output=$(script -q -c "bin/podman volume export testVol; echo \$?" /dev/null)
[arthur@localhost podman]$ line1=$(echo "$output" | sed -n '1p')
[arthur@localhost podman]$ line2=$(echo "$output" | sed -n '2p')
[arthur@localhost podman]$ echo $line1 | hexdump -C
00000000  45 72 72 6f 72 3a 20 72  65 66 75 73 69 6e 67 20  |Error: refusing |
00000010  74 6f 20 65 78 70 6f 72  74 20 74 6f 20 74 65 72  |to export to ter|
00000020  6d 69 6e 61 6c 2e 20 55  73 65 20 2d 6f 20 66 6c  |minal. Use -o fl|
00000030  61 67 20 6f 72 20 72 65  64 69 72 65 63 74 0d 0a  |ag or redirect..|
00000040
[arthur@localhost podman]$ echo $line2 | hexdump -C
00000000  31 32 35 0d 0a                                    |125..|
00000005
```
5. output and exit code will be like...
```
output=$(echo "$all_outputs" | sed -n '1p' | tr -d '\n\r')
exit_code=$(echo "$all_outputs" | sed -n '2p' | tr -d '\n\r')
```
**BATS test**
```sh
$ PODMAN=/home/arthur/podman/bin/podman QUADLET=/home/arthur/podman/bin/quadlet bats -T test/system/160-volumes.bats
...
 ✓ [160] podman volume export test in TTY [1447]
```


### Release Note
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

There are couples of test errors about `script` command not found, I need more time to look into Cirrus settings..
```
[+0199s] not ok 169 [160] podman volume export test in TTY in 929ms
         # (in test file test/system/[160-volumes.bats, line 629](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/f721bbb9961d68ced127bebbda7b7e9ab5676a88/test/system/160-volumes.bats#L629))
         #   `all_outputs=$(script -q -c "$PODMAN volume export testVol --output=$tarfile; echo \$?" /dev/null)' failed with status 127
         #
<+     > # # podman-remote volume rm -a
         #
<+044ms> # # podman-remote volume create testVol
<+040ms> # testVol
         #
<+010ms> # # podman-remote run -v testVol:/data --rm quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 sh -c echo hello > /data/myfile
         # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/system/160-volumes.bats: line 629: script: command not found
         #
<+390ms> # # podman-remote rm -a --volumes
         #
<+040ms> # # podman-remote volume rm -t 0 -a -f
<+037ms> # testVol
         # # [teardown]
```
You have a test failure. But code LGTM
I'd like to set this as a draft since I'm now trying to tune cirrus settings for fixing test failure.

Code change update:

### How I did it
1. When targetPath is empty and stdout is a terminal, print an error message: `cannot write to terminal, use command-line redirection or the --output flag` 
2. Refactoring: move `targetPath == ""` logic into the `else` branch 

### How to verify it
Add tests to `450-interactive.bats`
1. New Positive Case: podman volume export with output flag redirected to stdout by `>$PODMAN_TEST_PTY`.

2. New Negative Case: podman volume export without flags, redirected to stdout by `>$PODMAN_TEST_PTY`, fails as expected with the appropriate error message.

### BATS test
```sh
$ PODMAN=/home/arthur/podman/bin/podman QUADLET=/home/arthur/podman/bin/quadlet bats -T test/system/450-interactive.bats 
450-interactive.bats
...
 ✓ [450] podman volume export should fail when stdout is a tty [1609]

5 tests, 0 failures in 10 seconds
```
